title: Dryopteris microbasis
intro: Dryopteris microbasis, also known as the Japanese wood fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral activities, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris microbasis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
taxonomy: Dryopteris microbasis is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris, which consists of various species of wood ferns. The Dryopteris genus is part of the larger division of ferns, known as Polypodiophyta. Dryopteris microbasis is native to North America. It is classified as a terrestrial fern, growing in shaded areas.
